security_controls.evidence: routes/login.ts:34 — raw SQL with string interpolation; lib/insecurity.ts:43 — MD5 hash with no salt
security_controls.notes: Two compounding failures: (1) the login query is injectable via email field, bypassing the password check entirely; (2) when the query does evaluate, the stored hash is unsalted MD5 which is trivially reversible via rainbow tables. No bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt.

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:23 (hardcoded RSA private key), lib/insecurity.ts:54 (express-jwt@0.1.3 no algorithms allowlist), lib/insecurity.ts:56 (1024-bit RSA key)
security_controls.notes: Three compounding failures: (1) private key committed to public source repository; (2) express-jwt@0.1.3 has no algorithms parameter, enabling alg:none bypass; (3) 1024-bit key is below NIST minimum of 2048-bit.

security_controls.evidence: routes/2fa.ts — TOTP implementation present
security_controls.notes: TOTP is implemented and enforced when enabled for a user account, but it is optional and the underlying JWT forgery bypass makes 2FA irrelevant for attacker-minted tokens.

security_controls.evidence: Referenced in challenge data and Angular OAuth service; standard library flow
security_controls.notes: OAuth2 social login is present for Google accounts but relies on the same JWT infrastructure with the compromised private key.

security_controls.evidence: server.ts — 21 endpoints with missing_auth_suspect=true including admin endpoints
security_controls.notes: 21 endpoints that should require authentication do not. Includes /rest/admin/application-configuration, /rest/user/data-export, and file upload endpoints.

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:72 (in-memory tokenMap), lib/insecurity.ts:56 (6h expiry), lib/insecurity.ts:23 (hardcoded key)
security_controls.notes: Tokens are stored in localStorage (XSS-accessible). Server-side session is an in-memory map with no persistence or logout mechanism. The 6-hour expiry is the only session bound, but forged tokens can carry arbitrary expiry.

security_controls.evidence: No CSRF middleware found in server.ts; no SameSite cookie attribute set
security_controls.notes: No CSRF token or SameSite cookie protection. The app uses Authorization header JWT (not cookies) for most endpoints so the practical CSRF risk is reduced, but the cookie-based endpoints (profile) remain vulnerable.

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:54 (isAuthorized middleware); 18 routes with missing_authz_suspect; routes/address.ts IDOR pattern
security_controls.notes: JWT carries role claim (admin/customer) and isAuthorized() verifies token presence, but object-level authorization (IDOR) is missing across address, order, basket, payment, wallet, and memory endpoints. Any authenticated user can access any other user's objects.

security_controls.domain: Query Construction and Data Access Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/login.ts:34 — string interpolation; routes/search.ts:23 — LIKE injection; models use Sequelize ORM but raw() bypassed
security_controls.notes: Two confirmed raw SQL injection points. The ORM is available and used for most queries, but the security-critical paths (login, search) bypass it with raw string interpolation.

security_controls.evidence: sanitize-html@1.4.2 (outdated), no global validation middleware, routes/userProfile.ts:62 (eval on input)
security_controls.notes: Sanitization is inconsistent and point-in-time. sanitize-html@1.4.2 has known bypass CVEs. No global input schema validation middleware (joi/zod/express-validator absent). eval() on user input in userProfile.ts is a direct code injection vector.

security_controls.evidence: server.ts:187 (XSS filter commented out); routes/userProfile.ts:88 (user-controlled CSP header); Angular template rendering (partial protection)
security_controls.notes: Helmet's xssFilter is intentionally disabled. User-supplied profile image URL is injected into a Content-Security-Policy response header without sanitization, enabling stored XSS via CSP header injection. Angular provides some client-side protection but server-side is absent.

security_controls.evidence: server.ts:181-182 — app.options('*', cors()) + app.use(cors()) with no origin allowlist
security_controls.notes: Wildcard CORS allows any origin to make credentialed cross-origin requests. Combined with the missing CSRF protection, this enables CSRF-equivalent attacks from any web page.

security_controls.evidence: helmet.contentSecurityPolicy not called in server.ts; only per-response dynamic CSP in userProfile.ts
security_controls.notes: No application-wide CSP is set. The per-user CSP header in userProfile.ts is user-controlled and intentionally injectable (challenge design). No nonce-based script execution protection.

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:23 (RSA private key), lib/insecurity.ts:44 (HMAC key), server.ts:289 (cookie secret), routes/login.ts:61-66 (hardcoded credentials)
security_controls.notes: Four distinct secrets hardcoded in source: RSA private key, HMAC key, cookie parser secret, and multiple user plaintext credentials. All are committed to a public repository. No secrets management system (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, environment variables) is used.

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:43 — crypto.createHash('md5').update(data).digest('hex')
security_controls.notes: MD5 with no salt is used for all password hashing. MD5 is collision-prone, has no cost factor, and precomputed rainbow tables can reverse most common passwords in milliseconds.

security_controls.domain: File Parser and Outbound Request Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/fileUpload.ts:83 — libxmljs2.parseXml(data, { noent: true })
security_controls.notes: External entity resolution is explicitly enabled (noent: true). This allows XXE attacks that can read arbitrary files from the container filesystem, perform SSRF to internal network addresses, or cause denial of service.

security_controls.domain: File Parser and Outbound Request Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 — fetch(req.body.imageUrl) with no allowlist
security_controls.notes: The profile image URL endpoint fetches arbitrary URLs provided by the user without any allowlist, blocklist, or DNS rebinding protection. Enables SSRF to internal network resources and metadata endpoints.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.evidence: .npmrc sets package-lock=false; express-jwt@0.1.3 and jsonwebtoken@0.4.0 are critically outdated; no Dependabot
security_controls.notes: No lockfile and no automated dependency update tooling. Two critically outdated packages with known CVEs are pinned at ancient versions. npm install --unsafe-perm in Dockerfile runs postinstall scripts as root during build.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.evidence: Dockerfile — distroless image, UID 65532; CFG-001 (unpinned tags), CFG-004 (--unsafe-perm)
security_controls.notes: Runtime posture is good (distroless, non-root). Build-time posture is weak: base images are tag-pinned without digest, enabling supply-chain attacks via image mutation. npm install --unsafe-perm allows malicious postinstall scripts to run as root during build.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml (no permissions block), CodeQL SAST present but action refs unpinned
security_controls.notes: GitHub Actions workflows lack permissions restrictions (inherits write-all GITHUB_TOKEN). Action refs use mutable tags rather than SHA pinning. CodeQL SAST scanning is present and provides meaningful value. No release signing or SBOM provenance attestation in CI.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.evidence: server.ts:338 — morgan combined format to access log file
security_controls.notes: HTTP access logging via morgan covers all requests. No structured security event log (authentication failures, authorization denials, admin actions). No log integrity protection. Logs served via unauthenticated logfileServer endpoint with path traversal risk.

security_controls.evidence: lib/startup/registerWebsocketEvents.ts; socket.io@3.1.2
security_controls.notes: Socket.IO is used for real-time chat. The connection upgrade is authenticated by the same JWT middleware but individual socket event handlers do not re-validate the token on each message, creating a time-of-check/time-of-use gap if a token is revoked.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.assessment: Automated dependency updates not detected in the repository. Patch-management posture depends on this control being in place — the team is reactive rather than proactive without it.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.assessment: Lockfile hygiene not detected in the repository. Patch-management posture depends on this control being in place — the team is reactive rather than proactive without it.
